feed.title: Control and Automation for Intelligent Systems Lab
feed.subtitle: Research in the Control and Automation for Intelligent Systems Lab focuses on developing, analyzing, and applying control theory and artificial intelligence to improve the safety, flexibility, and performance of complex, dynamic systems, such as manufacturing systems.

entry.title: Penn State News for Digital Twins Hub Research and Mentorship

link.title: Penn State News for Digital Twins Hub Research and Mentorship

entry.content:
Dr. Kovalenko, one of the faculty participants in the ICDS Digital Twins Hub’s Faculty-in-Residence Program, and Ph.D. candidate Hongliang Li were featured in a recent Penn State News story. The article highlights their team’s work on the Water Evaluation and Treatment Digital Twin (WET-DT) project.

The project aims to develop a digital twin framework for more robust and predictive process control in water treatment systems.

Read more at the Digital Twins Hub’s Faculty-in-Residence Program highlights mentorship, research.
entry.summary: Dr. Kovalenko, one of the faculty participants in the ICDS Digital Twins Hub’s Faculty-in-Residence Program, and Ph.D. candidate Hongliang Li were featured in a recent Penn State News story. The article highlights their team’s work on the Water Evaluation and Treatment Digital Twin (WET-DT) project.

entry.title: Seminar at University of Iowa
entry.content:
Dr. Kovalenko delivered the seminar “Developing Intelligent Automation for Smart and Sustainable Manufacturing Systems” at the University of Iowa.

Read more at the Developing Intelligent Automation and Robotics for Smart Manufacturing Systems.
entry.summary: Dr. Kovalenko delivered the seminar “Developing Intelligent Automation for Smart and Sustainable Manufacturing Systems” at the University of Iowa.

entry.title: Welcome New Undergraduate Researchers
entry.content: Welcome to Sihan (Irene) and Jonathan, who joined the lab as undergraduate researchers!
entry.summary: Welcome to Sihan (Irene) and Jonathan, who joined the lab as undergraduate researchers!

entry.title: Farewell Bowling for Isabel
entry.content:
The lab held a farewell bowling event for Isabel, who is returning to the Technical University of Munich to complete her master’s degree.

We also celebrated the growing number of members in the Bowling 100 Points Club!
entry.summary: The lab held a farewell bowling event for Isabel, who is returning to the Technical University of Munich to complete her master’s degree.

entry.title: 2026 Manufacturing PA Expo
entry.content:
Ilya, Jiabao, and Jonghan attended the 2026 Manufacturing PA Expo in Harrisburg, PA, USA, where they showcased recent research on robotic assembly and engaged with the robotics, manufacturing, and automation community.

Congratulations to Jiabao on his outstanding performance at the 2026 Manufacturing PA Expo Poster Competition!
entry.summary: Ilya, Jiabao, and Jonghan attended the 2026 Manufacturing PA Expo in Harrisburg, PA, USA, where they showcased recent research on robotic assembly and engaged with the robotics, manufacturing, and automation community.

entry.title: Papers Accepted to WODES 2026 in Eindhoven, Netherlands

link.title: Papers Accepted to WODES 2026 in Eindhoven, Netherlands

entry.content:
Two papers have been accepted to the 18th Workshop on Discrete Event Systems (WODES) in Eindhoven, Netherlands.

The papers are titled “Logic-Based Verification of Task Allocation for LLM-Enabled Multi-Agent Manufacturing Systems” and “Attack-Resilient Control of Finite-State Cyber-Physical Systems via Inspection States.” Congrats Jonghan and Mostafa!
entry.summary: Two papers have been accepted to the 18th Workshop on Discrete Event Systems (WODES) in Eindhoven, Netherlands.

entry.title: ICDS Funds Mid-Scale Seed Grants
entry.content:
Dr. Kovalenko has received support through the Penn State Institute for Computational and Data Sciences (ICDS) Mid-Scale Seed Grant Program.

The project “Water Evaluation and Treatment Digital Twin (WET-DT): A Framework for Robust, Predictive Process Controls for Water Treatment Plants,” was selected as part of a new ICDS initiative supporting interdisciplinary research across the University. The project brings together collaborators from University Park and Penn State Behrend and contributes to ICDS research in digital twins and computational/data-driven science.

Read more at the Penn State Institute for Computational and Data Sciences (ICDS).
entry.summary: Dr. Kovalenko has received support through the Penn State Institute for Computational and Data Sciences (ICDS) Mid-Scale Seed Grant Program.

entry.title: Welcome New Undergraduate Researchers
entry.content: Welcome to Elijah Gruver and Chaitanya Patel, who joined the lab as undergraduate researchers!
entry.summary: Welcome to Elijah Gruver and Chaitanya Patel, who joined the lab as undergraduate researchers!

entry.title: Paper Accepted to ACC 2026
entry.content: Congratulations, Hongliang! The paper “Energy-Aware Integrated Predictive Maintenance Planning and Production Scheduling” has been accepted to the 2026 American Control Conference (ACC).
entry.summary: Congratulations, Hongliang! The paper “Energy-Aware Integrated Predictive Maintenance Planning and Production Scheduling” has been accepted to the 2026 American Control Conference (ACC).

entry.title: Paper Accepted to Robotics and Computer-Integrated Manufacturing

link.title: Paper Accepted to Robotics and Computer-Integrated Manufacturing

entry.content: Congratulations, Jonghan! The paper titled “Adaptive task planning and coordination in multi-agent manufacturing systems using large language models” has been accepted for publication in Robotics and Computer-Integrated Manufacturing.
entry.summary: Congratulations, Jonghan! The paper titled “Adaptive task planning and coordination in multi-agent manufacturing systems using large language models” has been accepted for publication in Robotics and Computer-Integrated Manufacturing.
